source: plan/bearings.md § Article hero art — colorful keyboard SVGs
template: directive: simple line drawing + single splash of color illustrative of subject
subject: Top-down silhouettes of two split keyboard halves (left and right, mirror images, column-staggered five-column-by-four-row main fields with three-key angled thumb clusters) sitting side-by-side with a meaningful center gap, overlaid by a single sage-green sparkline trend curve that travels from the lower-left of the left half, dips through the center gap (the split itself), then rises sharply through and above the right half; focal sage-green accent dot at the peak.
prompt: Third Trends-pillar hero in the series — must read as a sibling of trends-tracker-preview (ochre) and 75-percent-default (steel-blue) without colliding with either. Picked sage-green oklch(0.74 0.10 145) for the splash hue: it sits a full third of the hue wheel away from both prior pieces (75 ochre, 215 steel-blue, 145 sage forms a triadic-ish set), and 'green = growth' carries didactic weight for an article literally titled 'The split/ergo cohort grew up'. Composition fuses the brief's two requirements into one teaching frame: the split silhouettes ARE the chart's playing field, and the sparkline literally dips through the split before rising. Two halves at x=90→540 and x=660→1110 (~37.5% canvas width each), with a 120px center gap (x=540→660) — the gap is wide enough to read as 'split' but narrow enough that the two halves still feel like one keyboard, which is the whole point of the cohort. Each half: rounded case profile with an angled inner edge that hints at the inward tilt of a real ergo board (Corne, Kyria, Moonlander tradition); 5 columns × 4 rows of low-detail dots with column-stagger applied per-column (middle column highest, pinky lowest, mirroring real-finger length differences) — this is the unambiguous tell that distinguishes ergo from a row-staggered TKL silhouette; 3-key thumb cluster per side, rotated -12deg / +12deg respectively to angle inward toward the gap (the canonical ergo thumb-cluster signature). Faint column guide lines double as both row separators and chart gridlines, bridging the keyboard and chart readings (consistent with the 75% piece). Sparkline (11 points, vs. the 8-point sparklines of the prior two pieces — the extra points are needed to model the dip-and-rise) starts at (90, 540) inside the left half's lower-left, gently undulates across the left half, dips to its lowest point at (600, 564) — squarely in the center gap, the deepest point of the split — then rises sharply through the right half and peaks at (1110, 110) above the right case. The dip-then-rise shape literally illustrates the article's arc: the cohort was small/dismissed (left half, low and flat), the gap is the moment of doubt, and the right half is the growth phase. Single-splash convention preserved: low-fill area beneath the sparkline at 0.16 alpha, splash band wash at 0.08, sparkline stroke at full chroma 2.5px, focal cursor (filled core + outer ring) at the peak. Warm-grey 2px stroke for everything else mirrors the series voice. Aggregate inked content fills ~85% canvas width × ~75% canvas height, matching the rev-2 trends-tracker-preview rescaling.
